text: Type 2 diabetes, HbA1c 10.2%. On metformin 2g + glipizide. Initiating semaglutide 0.5mg weekly. Ophthalmology and podiatry referrals placed. Diabetic kidney disease stage G2A2.
Graves' disease, TSH undetectable, FT4 4.8. Methimazole 30mg daily initiated. Ophthalmology evaluation for thyroid eye disease. Radioactive iodine ablation discussed as definitive therapy.
Adrenal insufficiency confirmed by cosyntropin stimulation test (peak cortisol 8.2mcg/dL). Hydrocortisone 20mg AM / 10mg PM initiated. Stress dose instructions provided. MedicAlert bracelet advised.
Primary hyperparathyroidism. Calcium 11.4mg/dL, PTH 142pg/mL, 24hr urine calcium elevated. DEXA shows osteoporosis T-score -2.7. Parathyroid surgery referral placed.
Hypothyroidism, newly diagnosed. TSH 48.2, FT4 0.4. Levothyroxine 75mcg initiated. Repeat TSH in 6 weeks. Inquire about cardiac history before dose escalation in this 71-year-old patient.
Cushing syndrome, confirmed by 24hr UFC x2. MRI pituitary shows 6mm left microadenoma. Pituitary surgery referral placed. Interim ketoconazole for cortisol lowering.
Polycystic ovary syndrome, Rotterdam criteria met. Irregular cycles, hirsutism, BMI 33. Metformin initiated. Combined OCP for cycle regulation. Insulin resistance counseling provided.
Type 1 diabetes, DKA on presentation. pH 7.18, bicarbonate 9, glucose 622, gap 24. IV insulin drip initiated. Anion gap closure at 18 hours. Transitioned to basal-bolus insulin regimen.
Pheochromocytoma confirmed biochemically, normetanephrine 4x upper limit. CT adrenal shows 3.8cm right adrenal mass. Alpha blockade with phenoxybenzamine initiated prior to surgical resection.
Metabolic syndrome. BMI 38, BP 148/94, TG 380, HDL 32, fasting glucose 118. Lifestyle intervention initiated. Fenofibrate added. Discuss GLP-1 agonist at next visit.
